user_query:
I need to spin up three new service repos for a hack week and I want a consistent, production-ready scaffold for each. Read input/projects.yaml for the project list and specs. For each project, create a complete scaffold under output/projects/<project-name>/ following best practices for its language, including:
- .gitignore tuned to the language (must include common env files like .env)
- README.md with the project name as the H1, the description from the YAML, and clearly separated sections for Getting Started, Installation, Running, Testing, and Linting (include language-appropriate commands)
- A LICENSE file matching the license in the YAML (mit, apache2, or gpl3), with the current year and the author’s name, and .editorconfig at repo root
- CI config at .github/workflows/ci.yml targeting the specified CI provider (the YAML will set github for all three)
- Docker support if docker: true (Dockerfile, docker-compose.yml, and .dockerignore); if docker: false, do not include a Dockerfile
- Language-specific structure and files:
  - For Python: src/<module_name>/ with __init__.py, main.py, utils.py; tests/test_main.py; pyproject.toml; requirements.txt; requirements-dev.txt; setup.cfg; module_name should be the project name lowercased with dashes replaced by underscores
  - For Go: cmd/<project-name>/main.go; internal/app/app.go; pkg/utils/utils.go; go.mod; Makefile
  - For Rust: src/main.rs; src/lib.rs; tests/integration_test.rs; Cargo.toml
Also generate two aggregate artifacts:
- output/PORTFOLIO.md summarizing all projects in a single table (columns: Name, Language, License, Docker, CI, Path) with one row per project
- output/summary.json listing an array of objects with fields: name, language, license, docker (boolean), has_ci (boolean), path, and description
Use only the information in input/projects.yaml for names, descriptions, licenses, author, email, CI, and docker settings. Write all files directly (no external commands). Use only relative paths as specified above. Do not initialize git repos. Do not create any files outside output/.
